tagline: An unprofessional, unimportant, and undisciplined cabaret-style night of performance.
venueAddress: 317 East Houston Street, New York, NY 10002
accessibilityNote:
The bar is on the ground floor. The performance venue and accessible bathrooms are accessed via a portable ramp (1.5 steps).
Reserved seating available for accessibility needs upon request.
aboutText:
Unprofessional is a quarterly night of cabaret, where you never quite know what you'll get. Launched on Maya Suess's birthday in February 2022, the show lives in the back room of The Parkside Lounge on Houston and Attorney in the Lower East Side.

The programming is truly intergenerational, introducing younger performers to the legendary performers from the neighborhood. From Coney Island legends to drag kings, from performance artists to folk musicians, every edition is a different world.

pressLinks.title: EV Grieve - A visit to the Unprofessional Variety Show
Auntie Climax - Dance Party at Parkside

aboutDetails.title: The Venue
aboutDetails.text: The Parkside Lounge. A dive bar and performance venue catering to the artists, musicians, and freaks of the Lower East Side since 1908. On the corner of Houston and Attorney.

aboutDetails.title: Maya Suess
aboutDetails.text: Born on a small island off Canada's west coast (the unceded territory of the xʷməθkʷəy̓əm, Sḵwx̱wú7mesh, and səlil̓wətaʔɬ peoples), Maya now barely leaves NYC's Lower East Side in Lenapehoking. She gives money to NYC dancers by day and makes drawings, performances, and other mischievous entities by night.

aboutDetails.title: Klovis Gaynor
aboutDetails.text: Musical accompaniment at nearly every edition. Queer musician and "baroque punk" artist. Queens-based.

aboutDetails.title: The Band
aboutDetails.text: Libby Paloma, interdisciplinary artist and UpVS performer, makes our sets. Vandy, one half of the Vanburgo Collective, runs our tech, the other half, Lorenzo, shoots video. Tata Lopera takes photographs. Macauley Devun holds down the door when not on stage. The Parkside Lounge staff does sound, slings drinks, and keeps us in joyful chaos.

aboutDetails.title: The Looks
aboutDetails.text: Maya's looks are provided by Maegan and the team at East Village Vintage, on 12th and Avenue B. They have picked through jumbled second-hand racks to bring you a curated collection at the same low prices. Costumes have also been made/provided by Dylan Gong, Sarah Dahlinger, Tilly at VERS, Dew Igworia, and Jesse Harrod.

aboutDetails.title: The Deal
aboutDetails.text: Sliding scale cover. All monies go directly to the performers. Tickets through Humanitix, where 100% of booking fees go to charity.
